title: Moving to Montana from Greenwood, Wisconsin
description: Why they moved to Montana from Greenwood, Wisconsin. Husband didn't like dairying.

title: Homesteading south of Fort Peck; first flax crop; paying off their barn; Sunday baseball
description: Homesteading south of Fort Peck in 1910. First flax crop. Paying off their barn by giving dances on the hardwood floor of the loft; people came from great distances to dance . Sunday baseball.

title: The flu was hard on pregnant women; treatment by sweating
description: Hard work. The flu was especially hard on pregnant women; treatment by sweating.

title: Near death from slow consumption; a voice told her to get up from her bed
description: Her near death from slow consumption. A voice told her to get up from her bed and go home, and she did. The doctors called it a miracle.

title: Hard water; Wolf Point or Poplar; lost wheat in tree years of drought
description: Hard water. People went to town at Wolf Point or Poplar; the French Canadians had their own community. They lost their wheat in three successive years of drought.

title: Coming to Elk River; worked in hotel to get a room to live in; moved to the printing shop
description: Decision to come to Elk River. Superintendent Bloom would not let people loaf around town. She went to work in the hotel to get a room to live in, then moved over the printing shop. Japanese in town.

title: Business serving food at the hotel
description: The land-office business serving food at the hotel when the men struck in the woods (c.1920).

title: IWW's who helped them with harvest; rode freights to work on the harvest; hoboes stealing food from their basement
description: The IWW's who helped them with harvest in Montana were wonderful; they got barbed wire that the corporation had abandoned for the farm. People rode the freights west from Minnesota to work on the harvest; hoboes might steal food from their basement. At Elk River hoboes asked for food in return for work. IWWs were against the terrible camp conditions.

title: Moonshine sold to lumberjacks
description: Moonshine sold to lumberjacks. An excursion

title: Uncovering a stash of moonshine; town marshal in partnership; the hotel where she worked
description: Uncovering a stash of moonshine; the town marshal was in partnership. Three crews of men for the job. The hotel where she worked.

title: Cleaning up a bootlegging operation at a hotel
description: Cleaning up a bootlegging operation at a hotel.

title: Austrian moonshining in town; husband's work
description: Austrian moonshining in town. Charlie Segera, a fine Japanese, gave information about the mill to other Japanese. Husband's work.

title: Lumber piles persuaded her to board her home over the printing shop
description: Lumber pilers persuaded her to board them at her home over the printing shop. A Swede who wouldn't eat spinach; he was later killed in Seattle. How she was nicknamed "Ma" by her boarders.

title: Klan member suspected her of misconduct; burning crosses scared people; Klan clothes
description: A Klan member paid a visit to her home, suspecting her of misconduct. Burning crosses scared some people. Many were Southerners and related. Klan clothes hanging on the line to dry.

title: Shutting down the mill; husband refused to move; getting by in the depression
description: Shutting down the mill. Older Weyerhaeuser's great regret about plan to remove the mill from Elk River. Husband refused to move; closeness of people who remained. How they continued to make a living. Getting by in the depression - a huge haul of food from Juliaetta. Enjoyment of the country. Home.

title: Malker Anderson's suicide; Emil Anderson's death
description: Malker Anderson's suicide. Emil Anderson (his brother) died in a stump blast.

title: Malker's strange wife; Malker's son died in an accident at fifteen; made daughter clean the dishes
description: Malker's strange wife, Maggie May, was very cruel to her daughter. She helped Mrs. Vine take care of her ailing mother-in-law. Malker's son died in an accident at fifteen, an alcoholic. After her big parties she made her little daughter clean all the dishes.

title: Fatal plane crash; recipe for huckleberry ice cream
description: A fatal plane crash. George Schmaltz's stories about Harry Adams. Recipe for huckleberry ice cream.
